samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal to initiate multiplication. Multiplicand input. Multiplier input. Product output. Completion flag indicating the end of multiplication. Shift count register, 0 to 16. Update shift count register. if (!rst_n) i <= 0; else if (start && i < 17) i <= i + 1; else if (!start) i <= 0; Update completion flag. if (!rst_n) done_r <= 0; else if (i == 16) done_r <= 1; else if (i == 17) done_r <= 0; Update product register. if (!rst_n) yout_r <= 0; else if (start && i == 0) // Load operands begin areg <= ain; breg <= bin; yout_r <= 0; end else if (start && i > 0 && i < 17 && areg[i-1]) yout_r <= (breg << (i-1)) | (yout_r << 1); The module performs unsigned 16-bit multiplication using shift and accumulate operations. It maintains a shift count (i) to control the multiplication process and a completion flag (done_r) to indicate when the multiplication is done. On each clock cycle, if the reset signal is active, the shift count and completion flag are reset to zero. If the shift count is less than 17 and the start signal is active, the shift count increments by one. If the shift count is zero, the multiplicand and multiplier are loaded into the registers, and the product register is reset to zero. For shift counts greater than zero, if the corresponding bit in the multiplicand register is set, the multiplier register is shifted left by the shift count and appended to the least significant bits of the product register.
